com-b.label: What it takes to act
com-b.principle: Any action needs three things at once — the ability to do it, a real chance to, and motivation in the moment.

fogg-bmap.label: Make it easy and well-timed
fogg-bmap.principle: Behaviour happens when motivation, ability, and a prompt line up — the easier the action, the less motivation you need.

cbt-distortions.label: Check the thought
cbt-distortions.principle: Stuck feelings often ride on distorted thoughts (all-or-nothing, mind-reading, fortune-telling); naming and testing them loosens their grip.

sdt.label: Reconnect to your why
sdt.principle: Motivation lasts when an action feels self-chosen and tied to what you value, not forced on you.

implementation-intentions.label: If-then planning
implementation-intentions.principle: Deciding in advance exactly when and where you will act sharply raises follow-through.

motivational-interviewing.label: Talk yourself toward it
motivational-interviewing.principle: Hearing your own reasons for change — in your own words — builds more commitment than being told.

self-compassion.label: Be kind to yourself
self-compassion.principle: Meeting a slip with kindness instead of harsh self-criticism makes you more likely to get back on track.

fear-setting.label: Name the worst case
fear-setting.principle: Writing out what you fear, and how you would cope, shrinks vague dread into something you can handle.

temptation-bundling.label: Pair it with a treat
temptation-bundling.principle: Linking a habit you should do with something you enjoy makes you more likely to actually do it.

habit-stacking.label: Anchor to an existing habit
habit-stacking.principle: Attaching a new habit right after one you already do gives it a reliable, built-in cue.

environment-design.label: Design your surroundings
environment-design.principle: Making the good choice the easy, obvious one — and distractions harder — beats relying on willpower.

two-minute-rule.label: Two-minute start
two-minute-rule.principle: Shrinking a habit to two minutes lowers the cost to begin — and starting is most of the battle.

five-second-rule.label: Move in five seconds
five-second-rule.principle: Counting down 5-4-3-2-1 and moving interrupts hesitation before your brain talks you out of it.

zeigarnik.label: Just start the loop
zeigarnik.principle: Unfinished tasks nag at the mind — once you begin, the pull to finish works in your favour.

loss-aversion.label: Protect what you have built
loss-aversion.principle: We feel a loss about twice as strongly as a gain, so not breaking a chain you have built is a powerful pull.

timeboxing.label: Short focused sprints
timeboxing.principle: Single-tasked sprints with a clear end protect attention and make starting feel safe.

no-cue.label: It slips my mind
no-cue.tell: I just forget or never get to it.

too-much-friction.label: Too much hassle
too-much-friction.tell: It’s just a hassle to get going.

no-momentum.label: Waiting to feel like it
no-momentum.tell: I’ll start once I feel like it.

boredom.label: It’s boring
boredom.tell: It’s so dull I avoid it.

fear-of-failure.label: Fear of failing
fear-of-failure.tell: What if I try and fail?

fear-of-judgment.label: Fear of being judged
fear-of-judgment.tell: People will judge me for this.

uncertainty.label: Not knowing how it’ll go
uncertainty.tell: I don’t know how this will turn out.

overwhelm.label: It feels too big
overwhelm.tell: It feels too big to even face.

imposter.label: I’m not cut out for this
imposter.tell: I’m not really cut out for this.

not-deserving.label: I don’t deserve it
not-deserving.tell: I don’t deserve to do this for myself.

comparison.label: Everyone’s ahead of me
comparison.tell: Everyone else is so far ahead.

self-criticism.label: I always mess up
self-criticism.tell: I always mess this up anyway.

perfection-worth.label: It has to be perfect
perfection-worth.tell: If it’s not perfect it doesn’t count.

no-proof-yet.label: Nothing to show yet
no-proof-yet.tell: I’ve got nothing to show for it.

tried-before.label: It didn’t stick before
tried-before.tell: I’ve tried before and it didn’t last.

all-or-nothing.label: All or nothing
all-or-nothing.tell: If I can’t do it fully, why bother?

overthinking.label: Stuck in my head
overthinking.tell: I keep analysing instead of doing.

task-aversion.label: I just don’t want to
task-aversion.tell: I really don’t want to start.

waiting-to-feel-ready.label: Waiting to feel ready
waiting-to-feel-ready.tell: I’ll start when I feel ready.

present-bias.label: Something funner right now
present-bias.tell: I’d rather do something fun right now.

vague-plan.label: Not sure where to start
vague-plan.tell: I’m not sure where to begin.

fixed-mindset.label: Too old to change
fixed-mindset.tell: I’m too old to change now.

sunk-cost.label: Left it too late
sunk-cost.tell: I’ve left it too late to matter.

low-energy-age.label: Not the energy I had
low-energy-age.tell: I don’t have the energy I used to.

comparison-young.label: Everyone’s younger
comparison-young.tell: Everyone starting this is younger than me.

q-smallest.text: What’s the smallest version of this you couldn’t say no to?

q-one-step.text: What’s the very first physical step — just step one?

q-tiny-promise.text: Could you promise just two minutes, and stop after if you still want to?

q-5min-change.text: What will actually be different in five minutes that isn’t true right now?

q-when-where.text: Exactly when and where will you do this today?
q-when-where.followUp: Picture the moment — it makes it far more likely to happen.

q-after-what.text: Which thing you already do every day could this come right after?

q-evidence.text: What’s the real evidence for that thought — and what’s the evidence against it?

q-worst-case.text: If the worst happened, how would you actually handle it?

q-name-fear.text: If you name the fear out loud, what is it really about?

q-future-self.text: What would the person you’re becoming do right now?

q-why-matters.text: Why did this matter enough to start in the first place?

q-advice-friend.text: What would you say to a friend who felt exactly this way?

q-deserve.text: If you did deserve this, what would you do next?

q-cost-of-skip.text: What does skipping today quietly cost you?

q-treat-pair.text: What could you pair this with so it’s something to look forward to?

q-remove-friction.text: What one thing could you remove or set up to make this easier?

q-whose-voice.text: Whose voice is that “you can’t” — and is it telling the truth?

q-too-late.text: If not now, when does “too late” actually begin?

q-proof.text: What small experiment would show whether this really works for you?

q-feel-after.text: How will you feel about yourself ten minutes after you’ve started?

q-perfection.text: Does this need to be perfect, or does it just need to be done?

q-energy-now.text: What version of this fits the energy you actually have right now?

q-past-win.text: When have you pushed through something like this before?

q-reasons.text: Give yourself three honest reasons this is worth doing today.

q-resist-what.text: What are you actually resisting — the task, or a feeling about it?

two-minute-rule.name: The two-minute start
two-minute-rule.how: Promise yourself just two minutes. Begin the tiniest first action and let momentum take over.

mini-version.name: Shrink the goal
mini-version.how: Cut it down to a version so small it feels almost silly to skip — one page, one set, one line.

five-second-rule.name: Five-second launch
five-second-rule.how: Count 5-4-3-2-1 and physically move before the hesitation can grow.

if-then.name: If-then plan
if-then.how: Lock in the exact cue: “When I [finish X / it’s 7pm], then I will [do this] here.”

habit-stack.name: Stack it on a habit
habit-stack.how: Attach it right after something you already do every day, so the old habit becomes the cue.

environment-design.name: Set the stage
environment-design.how: Remove one bit of friction and put the cue in plain sight — make starting the path of least resistance.

temptation-bundle.name: Pair it with a treat
temptation-bundle.how: Only let yourself enjoy something you love (a show, a podcast) while you do this.

pomodoro.name: A focus sprint
pomodoro.how: Set a short timer, single-task until it rings, then take a real break. One sprint is enough to start.

reframe-thought.name: Test the thought
reframe-thought.how: Write the harsh thought down, then list the evidence for and against it. Replace it with a fairer one.

self-compassion-break.name: A kindness break
self-compassion-break.how: Say what you’d tell a struggling friend: this is hard, you’re human, and one small step is enough.

fear-setting.name: Name the worst case
fear-setting.how: Write the worst that could happen, how likely it is, and how you’d recover. Watch the dread shrink.

values-anchor.name: Reconnect to your why
values-anchor.how: Finish this out loud: “I’m doing this because it matters to me, since…” Let the reason pull you.

reasons-out-loud.name: Three reasons, your words
reasons-out-loud.how: Say three honest reasons this is worth doing today. Hearing your own case builds real commitment.
